title: DSDS Status Definition
description: Lifecycle status of a design system artifact with optional per-platform readiness tracking. The `status` union def accepts a plain statusValue string (e.g., "stable") or a full statusObject when platform-specific tracking or deprecation notices are needed. The statusObject holds overall status, per-platform readiness, and deprecationNotice in one reusable object; deprecationNotice is required when status is 'deprecated'.

statusValue.description: A lifecycle status value. MUST be lowercase kebab-case. Standard values: 'draft' (under development, not ready for use), 'experimental' (available but API may change without notice), 'stable' (production-ready, changes follow semver), 'deprecated' (scheduled for removal). Custom values are permitted (e.g., 'sunset', 'archived', 'beta') and MUST follow the same pattern.

platformStatus.description: Readiness status of an artifact on a specific platform. Tracks lifecycle stage, version availability, and optional deprecation notices per platform.

status.description: Lifecycle status of the artifact on this platform. Uses the same vocabulary as the top-level status: 'draft', 'experimental', 'stable', 'deprecated'. Custom values are permitted.

since.description: The version in which this artifact became available on this platform.

deprecationNotice.description: Required when status is 'deprecated'. Explains what to use instead on this platform.

description.description: Notes about the artifact's status on this platform (e.g., 'Available as a Web Component wrapper; native implementation planned for v4').

statusObject.description: Consolidated lifecycle status of an artifact. Holds the overall status value, optional per-platform readiness tracking, and a conditional deprecation notice. All status-bearing artifacts use this single object instead of separate status, platformStatus, and deprecationNotice siblings. When overall status is 'deprecated', deprecationNotice becomes required.

overall.description: Overall lifecycle status of the artifact. Represents an editorial judgment about maturity — not required to match the most or least advanced platform status. Tools SHOULD display this as the primary status indicator.

platforms.description: Per-platform readiness or availability status. Keys are platform identifiers (freeform strings — common values include 'react', 'web-component', 'ios', 'android', 'flutter', 'figma', 'sketch', and 'compose'). Values describe the artifact's status on that platform. An artifact MAY be 'stable' overall while individual platforms are still 'experimental' or 'draft'.

deprecationNotice.description: Required when status is 'deprecated'. MUST explain what to use instead and provide a migration path. Tools SHOULD display this prominently alongside the status indicator.

status.description: Lifecycle status of an artifact. Accepts a plain status string (e.g., "stable", "deprecated") for the simple case, or a full status object with per-platform tracking and deprecation notices. Tools SHOULD normalize the string form to { "overall": value } for consistent processing.
